reviews.review_description:
First play through and I loved it, rewlly got a quick feel for the controls and loved the diversity of the enemies and bosses, one issue I must say is that multitasking is a must, not that’s it’s awful and I hate it, but it’s understandable knowing what and who you deal with.
One last thing to mention, the music is so amazing, while playing I got hooked with it, if anyone didn’t read the description or can’t find it, the person who helped with the music was the “Fat Bard.” I can’t stop glazing it.

developer_response.body: Hey Irradiant_Flame, thanks for the kind word!  Multitasking is definitely a must.  And Fat Bard is actually a musical duo who've we've had the pleasure of working with for several projects now!  You can find their soundtrack to Runner as well our other games (Rogue Flight, 2MD, Akash) on Spotify and SoundCloud!

reviews.review_description: They don't have femboys

This is a great game and I really enjoy the motorcycle aspect. I enjoy visuals and the combat nature . The only negative that I can give this game is that I feel it's a missed opportunity because you are riding a motorcycle and you don't hold the handles . That's the only negative I can give it.

developer_response.body:
Hey there!

Thanks for the feedback and for giving the game a go.  Would you believe that, from the outset, until maybe half way through development, holding the handles was EXACTLY how the game played?

Turns out, people are not so good with holding onto motorcycle handles while trying to multitask shooting enemies and weaving through traffic.  in testing, this proved to be incredibly frustrating for both novice and experienced players, hence the hybrid control scheme we eventually adopted.

this is one of those cases where realism HAD to be sacrificed for playability, as a limitation of both the technology and human situational awareness kind of resulted in a worse experience for it.

reviews.review_description: Before, in version 1.0.0 it had more difficulty, which made everything more exciting, now it is a game for children under 5 years old

developer_response.body:
Not sure what the heck you’re talking about. 

We didn’t decrease the difficulty, we added additional modes, fix some bugs, added new stages, and improved performance. We also added a checkpoint system that’s completely optional for you to use.

reviews.review_description: It's motorcycle carnage at its finest, blasting through the authorities has never been more fun, but one I would like to see is a free roam sandbox mode, it would be so much fun riding around the city destroying everything, but all in all a great bike destruction game, it's a retro style so the old school gamers would really appreciate this game
I love playing this game while listening to music.

developer_response.body: Thanks so much for the kind word! I appreciate it, and I hope you’re able to explore all of the additional music we packed into the game :)

reviews.review_title: This game is so bloody COOL!!!
reviews.review_description:
This game was made in a LAB to be the sickest, most radical VR game EVER.  This the type of game Uzi from Murder Drones would play, and that’s a compliment!!!!! The artstyle of this game is so cool, the combat is awesome, the enemies are plentiful and in great variety, AND YOUR CHARACTER HAS A ROBOTIC METAL ARM!!!!!!!

PLEASE keep cooking, devs, and if you haven’t bought this game already, BUY IT. BUY IT NOW!!! I’M NO LONGER ASKING!!!!

reviews.review_title: This game has grown much since release
reviews.review_description:
Did not like it at all when it first came out, but it is so much better now.
You can customize the settings to further increase immersion when steering btw.
I've had a really good time with this game, although I am yet to figure out the reason to use the power sword as I feel it activates and deactivates to sluggishly as this is a really fast paced game.
I would have loved if stages had more ultra speed sections!
And being able to jump on ramps would have been awesome, but I can see why it was never implemented (nausea).
But why nitpick, this game is 100% what makes vr shine. It nails every aspect and has this just one more go loop.
Love it and would commit crimes for a sequel.
One big letdown is that the game crashes frequently on my quest 3, but what the heck. 5 stars it is!

And lastly, charged shot is way too overpowered
Cringed to death. المرسال "pukes in Arabic"

developer_response.body:
Er, you wrote ‘cringe’ three times? I have it on good word that that’s pretty cringe? And yet you failed to specify what was cringe about the game??

Joking aside- Sorry you didn’t like the game, hope you find something that suits your tastes! مع السلامة!

reviews.review_description:
UPDATE: cloud backup causes file corruptions. Still love the game

The reason I put three stars is because of cloud backup. I once had to factory reset my headset and I had backups of almost everything,but your game said it doesn’t have a cloud backup. I had so much progress and for reference I was at the train level, which is very hard btw, and after the factory reset all my progress was gone. Please, just add. Cloud backup please .

developer_response.body:
Cloud backup unfortunately has a high probability of causing file corruption; the game originally had it at launch and we found that every time there was a platform update it corrupted people’s saves, so we had to disable it on the platform level. It really ruined many more people’s experiences and it took a lot of debugging to figure out what was going on. I agree that it’s a great convenience to have (especially in your situation) but it’s not a core aspect of the game experience in this case. 

It might come back at a later time, but I’m sorry for the inconvenience regardless.  Please keep that in mind should you decide to revisit your review?

reviews.review_description: This game is fun AF. Great game play mechanics and graphics. Amazing music too😁🔥

developer_response.body: Hey JaviC12!  Thanks for the kind word - I'm glad you dig it!

reviews.review_description: I only played for a few minutes but already love it. Will go back to it when time allows. It's very colourful and interactive, great sound effects. I have to get used to using the weapons but it seems not too difficult to become accustomed to. Not having to hold handlebars at the same time is a huge advantage too. Definitely recommend it!

developer_response.body: Hey Maccyb123! Thanks for the kind word, and I’m really glad you enjoyed what you’ve played so far!

reviews.review_description: Great atmosphere and good gameplay.

developer_response.body: Hey yharif, thanks for the kind word!  very glad to see.

The speed the combat the adrenalin I get from this game is insane-! I love Mina a lot and I really wanna know more about the characters! The style of this game is beautiful and nostalgic and for those who love a challenge Id recommend this game

(Off topic from the game devs please read-)

reviews.review_description: Cyberpunk anime experience in VR with all bells and whistles

developer_response.body: Hey creeper2004!  Thanks for the kind word, I truly appreciate it.

reviews.review_description: This is ace.  Ranks right up there with the best arcade games the Quest has to offer.  If Sega was still making games, seeing their name on this wouldn't be amiss.  For once the headset actually aids the immersion, helping you live out what you imagined anime bike combat would be.  Ten stages in total with each having a variant mode that lets you see the sights combat-free and pick from one of the 30 music tracks in the game.  For when you want a more casual experience, there is a toggle to tone down damage taken and one where you can take infinite damage.  Four bikes to unlock, a slew of ammo types to grab during a run for both your hand and bike weapons, and your bike weapon has numerous combinations a la Gunstar Heroes.  You can even play one-handed if you really need to.  And just because it's cool, achievements and Easter eggs galore.  Oh, and full Japanese voice acting is an option!  (Which as an aside, is also a bit of an Easter egg.)

developer_response.body: Hey Archangel_01!  Thanks for the kind word, I really appreciate it.  you also totally nailed all of the references:  Sega Arcade racing was a major influence, and it's always gratifying to see folks enjoying the Japanese option as well.

reviews.review_description:
Runner is a fast and stylish game that makes you feel like you’re inside a classic cyberpunk anime. You ride a bike through neon city streets, dodging traffic and shooting enemies, all while a great synth soundtrack plays in the background.

The bike controls feel natural, and the mix of driving and shooting is fun and smooth. The visuals are bright and retro-futuristic, which look great in VR.

The game isn’t very long, but it’s easy to replay and improve your score, which keeps it fun. If you like arcade-style action with speed and style, Runner VR is a great choice.

developer_response.body: Hey Axgarr! thanks for the kind words, we really appreciate it!  I'm glad you enjoyed the game :)

reviews.review_description: Super fun and intuitive to play.

developer_response.body: Hey Jackson, Many thanks! I'm glad yo enjoyed it!

reviews.review_description: SUCH A WINNER!

developer_response.body: Hey Qipper!  Thanks for the kind word!  We appreciate it!

reviews.review_description:
The game is ace, BUT I have done zone 3 SO many times now and stage 4 wont unlock. Unless im doing something wrong then it's a 5 stars. Love the game but its no good to me atm. Please help. 😊

EDIT, The developer got back to me really fast, less than 12 hours. It was my fault. I wasnt unlocking level 4. I now give it 5 STARS. Its an excellent game. Love it. Thanks for the quick response.

developer_response.body:
Edit:  Hey Worzel76!  Thank you for the kind words and for coming back to revise your review!  I'm glad we were able to get it rectified - honestly I think we will work harder in the future to make sure main modes and alternative modes are presented with better clarity and context.

previous response below:

Hey there! 

So I’m hoping you read this and revise your review, because I think you actually missed the main play mode.  😅

To play through the game and unlock more stages you need to play through the NEW GAME / story mode. It sounds like you’re playing stuff in LEVEL SELECT. That only has three stages unlocked at the start (all bonus stages) because you have to complete the main game to unlock the rest of them For replay later. You need to pick the option on the left to start a new game, not the option on the right which is level select

There aren’t zones to play through, the main game will take you linearly through the story and unlock stages for level select as you beat them

reviews.review_description: Game is simple. Reminds me of swarm. although I throughly recommend buying I have some negative things to say. The enemy’s as of stage 5 is a bit repetitive though it feels fine. The voice acting is a bit repetitive and could use work but I can’t judge much as I’m no expert. Despite that I it is worth its price and I only ended my first and only session because my head set was dying.

developer_response.body:
Hey heddg,
Thanks for the kind review!   I'm glad you ended up enjoying the game, and I hope you find more to enjoy as you unlock stages and modes!

reviews.review_description: I just finished the game and it is a fast pace, shooter game that I really like plus when I saw the final boss, the first thing I said is holy Transformers ￼￼￼

developer_response.body: Hey Trevorleonard2013! Thanks for the kind words, and I agree with you on the final boss, though I would also have accepted 'holy Gundam' as well ;)

reviews.review_description: For the sale price of $7.50, this is INCREDIBLE. Even at $15 it’s worth every penny. I played the 15 min trial and it was an IMMEDIATE purchase. It’s very simple overall, but just great to play through.

developer_response.body: Hey PhantomIntelligence!  Thanks for the kind words and the recommendation!  I'm really glad you're digging the game.

reviews.review_description:
I didn't think this game would be as awesome as it looked but boy was I wrong. I played a trial, and got immediately hooked. The presentation, performance and inmersion is fantastic. You really feel you're in a cyberpunk anime being an action hero fighting bots with your weapons and cool bike. 

Zero nausea and movement was very intuitive. Highly recommend this game.

developer_response.body:
Hey alclab!
Thanks for the kind word.  Glad to see the game lived up to the expectations cause there's a LOT of awesome games on the store, so we definitely appreciate the kind word.  Also glad to see we were able to keep the user discomfort to a minimum, which was quite a challenge for a game like this, heh.

reviews.review_title: It's all my favorite arcade games in one
reviews.review_description: It's Spy Hunter on a friggin motorcycle! It's Road Blasters fused with Hang On! It's Freedom Fighter fused with Road Blaster! It's Terminator 2! It's Akira! It's five effing stars! Theres is no way this game could possibly be better, get it.

developer_response.body: Hey there rmzalbar!  Thanks for the kind words, and I'm digging that you got the Road Blasters inspiration as well!

reviews.review_description:
The only thing that kills me for this game are the controls.

Pressing a to pick up your gun in vr is anti immersive.

The fact that you have to control the brick with the joysticks instead of leaning give motionsickness

If the game had modern vr controls it would be a 5 star masterpiece.

developer_response.body:
Hey there! I’m sorry you didn’t like the controls, and while it’s disappointing that you felt we deserved such a relatively low rating, we understand you can’t please everyone! Still, as a bit of an explanation: we worked extremely hard on the control system to try and create some thing that was accessible and responsive. The honest truth is, the “modern VR controls“ that you speak up are not ideal for a high-speed, frenetic, 360° experience on a motorcycle. In fact, during testing, this induced more disorientation and motion sickness for players because they had to constantly take their eyes off the road and off of the enemies to navigate the control panel, draw, and dock, the pistols, and reach for grenades. This is why we created a hybrid system that allows you to use hand movements to point, and shoot, but reduces the need for fumbling while taking high-speed turns. Being able to draw and dock the pistols without having to look for the holster, while driving and dodging enemies goes along way toward mitigating discomfort. 

We’ve included options to  reduce  motion sickness in the settings menu. I suggest you give it a shot, but I will emphasize that it is likely not the controls that are causing you any discomfort.  Remember, VR is a relatively new technology, there aren’t really standards and practices for one type of experience or another with regards to how controls are implemented. It’s largely a frontier, and as an indie developer, you have to build everything from scratch.

I suppose all I can ask, is that you give the game another chance, look at it from our perspective, and if you can adapt to the experience we’ve created, please reconsider your review :) !

reviews.review_description: Stylish game... but I am prefer to win, but not be defeated.

developer_response.body: Hey there! Good news: there’s an easy mode and an invincible mode. Please give those a try and update your review :)

reviews.review_title: Good concept, bully game to player
reviews.review_description: It looks really cool and sounds really good I’ll give this game that but this is one of those games where they leave you in the dark but make it extremely difficult to figure out. It’s a game where you shoot an enemy 1000 times but one hit does half your life, and then when the shield you get after you defeat an enemy doesn’t even bounce near you and when you swerve to get it you miss it. On top of when you swerve to miss an enemy’s bullet it’ll just track you and follow you. This game has enemy’s where at points they’re invincible and then other points weak but you never know when the time to shoot or not. If you got the time to play at night to devote an hour to figure out one level then this game is for you. It was fun at first but the whole figuring out a level and then fighting a near impossible boss that seems to just get stronger and you can’t avoid the “blast radiuses” got annoying. If you’re gonna have multiple enemy types, put a guide.

developer_response.body:
Hey bud.  I'm sorry to see you didn't have a good time.  It's important to acknowledge that every player's experience is theirs, and there's not 'wrong' impressions.  However, after digging into your review, you've got some takes here that we probably need to address here:

Firstly, I promise we don't leave players in the dark.  There's a reasonably extensive tutorial at the beginning of the game that not only familiarizes you with your bike and the weapons, it clues you in on how to defeat enemies.  Just about every enemy in the game has a flashing weakpoint (a trope inspired by old school games) that you need to focus fire on. You don't need to shoot any of the enemies "1000" times, though you need to focus your efforts and think a little more tactically in many situations:  Enemies are tiered in such a way that the easiest enemies have their weakpoints exposed (and usually can be destroyed with a couple shots), whereas harder ones require you chip away at their armor to reveal it. (the clue being the bright yellow armor that can only be destroyed by your bike cannon, missiles or grenades)

Second, I can assure you that bullets don't "track and follow you".  Believe it or not, that would be computationally expensive and the Quest doesn't have that kind of overhead.  you've got some good maneuverability that you'll need to use in order to weave around enemy shots; you can also pull your saber and (if you time it right) destroy and deflect incoming fire.  Every enemy telegraphs that they are about to fire well before they start, and it is usually a simple matter of getting out of the way before the let loose.  Some weapons, like explosives and missiles, do have a wider area of effect, but they are also designed to be shot out of the sky, again with a fairly lengthy period to allow you to do so.  No weapons in the game 'take half your health', not even the final boss has that kind of ordinance.  I suspect you might be getting overwhelmed because, again, it's all about the approach.

Reading your review, I think the core of the issue is like alot of gamers you may  be accustomed to games that provide a great deal more guidance or scripting to their encounters.  This isn't a bad thing, it's just not what our game is about: RUNNER is a decidedly retro-inspired arcade experience, and half the fun is figuring out HOW to defeat these enemies, and incorporating that into your greater strategy.  That's where a great deal of the sense of accomplishment comes from in these kinds of experiences.  Almost like a Souls' game.

That being said - we've actually incorporated alot of early feedback similar to yours into the last few updates, and I suspect you're maybe not taking advantage of any of the additional assists we've added to the game since launch:  First, there are hints all over the place, from the dead-drops between levels to the restart screen, giving you clues how to approach encounters.  Second, there is a 'free play' mode, which removes the failstate but keeps everything else the same.  this is essentially the 'easy' mode; the idea here is, allow you to experience the game fully without threat of losing.  Additionally, when you finish any stage (be it in regular mode or with free play on) it's unlocked in level select for you to re-play.  you can then take on the stage again any time you want without free play turned on, to try and conquer it without the assist, if that's your thing, and earn all the achievments, unlocks, and leaderboard ranks that come with it.  So you've got plenty of options to tune the difficulty and the experience to your liking.  We are also adding bonus stages to level select, each with their own quirks and challenges.

Lastly, asking for a guide is an interesting query - I had considered some kind of codex with snippets on each enemy class at some point in the game, however it's not something that is easily shoehorned into the experience.  And again, part of the joy of the game is the discovery: learning the enemy patterns and figuring out their weaknesses.

So, in the end, while I understand that the default challenge may not be your thing, there are additional assists and modes of play you should probably consider.  We're not in the business of frustrating players, rather we just want to challenge them (cause that's where the fun is).  Again, sorry you didn't have a good experience, I hope the tips above are helpful.  take care.

reviews.review_description:
Runner is one of the best run and gun games around. It's a frenetic experience not for the faint of heart. I would recommend people to first get acquainted with the VR experience overall, as this game is not for beginners in VR.

 If you're just starting in VR, probably this game will give you motion sickness. Don't give up on it, you'll get the hang of things after several play sessions.

developer_response.body:
Hey Jrvizca!  Thanks for the kind word and the review!  You pretty much encapsulated the experience perfectly!

For what it's worth, we have been incredibly fortunate, most users (even sensitive players) don't really seem to have any issues with motion sickness as we took some very deliberate steps to mitigate that in the game's design (we have one tester in particular who is suuuper sensitive, so he was our litmus test throughout early development!) .  

Still, you're definitely right as it is a moderately intense experience, so we've given people tools in the options to mitigate any issues that may arise.  Thanks again, and take care!

reviews.review_description:
Very fun game. I have to agree with most here, however, in that the boss levels are something else. The levels to get to the boss are pretty easy when the RNG doesn’t crush you. The bosses, however, are long encounters where a couple of hits takes you down, back to the beginning of the level. A checkpoint before the boss would be welcome, because again the lead up to the boss isn’t hard. It just takes an incredible amount of time when you have to keep trying for a boss. Don’t know if I’ll see past the third stage because I don’t care for playing on godmode and I don’t care for playing the preamble over and over. I’ll check it out again in a few months and update my review then.
Unrelated: “retro hard” difficulty was always bad and only existed to eat quarters at the arcade and pad game time at home.

developer_response.body: Hey there! we've got checkpoints for mid-stage and for boss encounters coming in the next update, so you won't need to wait a few months, just a few days.  "Retro hard" difficulty applies not just to quarter munching arcade games, but also 16 and 32 bit games of the era, so it's not merely a means to empty your pockets :) The current setup was intended to offer a challenge, but not impart any frustration. So, we are happy to oblige folks wanting additional options, all we ask is your patience to make said changes carefully and effectively.

reviews.review_title: 5 star fun but need help at the end
reviews.review_description:
This is a sure fire 5 star game for me, bought it when it came out and loved it until I just couldn't get past the final boss, and needing some help. 

At the end of the game is a giant boss with swords but I absolutely cannot seem to beat this guy and was looking for a solution. I have gone in with lasers and die everytime. If you restart then you lose your weapons anf it downgrades. When it restarts but the same thing, die everytime. Is there a special way to beat that last boss? Then what sucks is if you decide to quit there is NO save spot before the final boss so you're forced to play level 6 over again, which I've done maybe 5 times at least. 

Would love a save point before the final boss added to an update for the game or a solution on what to do to beat the final boss.

developer_response.body:
Hey UHATEIT!  Thanks for the kind word!  Sorry to hear you're having issues with the last boss.  After reading your description, I was curious - have you grabbed the last couple of updates to the game?  the 1.2 update actually added a save point right before the boss (so you don't have to play through level 6 again if you lose while fighting him).  

As far as defeating him, there isn't really a special trick, you just have to bombard his chest plate as much as possible.  Any weapon will work (the laser is a good choice, but really any weapon even the basic autocannon will do.  the only one I'd advise against using is maybe the spread shot / shotgun, as that has a limited range). Once it's flashing red, you'll know you've achieved enough damage, and at that point you just have to survive until he tries to use his chest laser.  Once the plate opens, fire into it as he's charging up, and you'll defeat him! 

An alternative is, switch to 'free play' mode in the options menu before you continue again, which makes you invincible.  you will still have to do enough damage to him before he fires his laser at you in order to defeat him, but you won't have the added worry of trying to survive.  Plus, even if you beat the game in invincible mode, it will unlock the stage in level select and you can replay it in regular mode and gain all the achievements :)

good luck!  also please feel free to join our discord (got to our twitter account @runner_vr, and click on the link in our profile). We'll be happy to help you there if you need as well!

reviews.review_description: It reminds me of racings games of old but in VR with guns, def recommend.

developer_response.body: Hey Catfish_Jones1, thanks for the kind word!  That's a great descriptor for what we were aiming for!

reviews.review_description:
Love this game. It's so fun to drive and fire at your pursuers or get close to other bikes and cut them with your sword. Totally recommend this one for anime and rail shooter fans that want to experience their genres' in VR. 

M Q 3 . B I O . L I N K (Just copy or type without the spaces)

The gameplay reminds me of Star Fox or Panzer Dragoon paired with a first person shooter. If you're not steering, your bike automatically stays in the same "lane", which lets you turn around and point your hand at any enemies surrounding you. There are collectable powerups on the road that give you new weapons, risky manoeuvres are being awarded and bosses are challenging. One of the Quest games I come back for the most. 

Can't praise this one enough. Thanks to the developers for creating thus pearl. 

If this was in any way helpful to you, please consider giving it a 👍 up.

developer_response.body:
Hey Brettspalter,

Thanks for the kind word! We’re glad you dig the game.

reviews.review_description:
The game is intense, graphics are beautiful, the music was amazing, the way the game makes your adrenaline rush is insane, and I like the small story that you get to experience. Overall the best game I’ve played that actually got my adrenaline rushing. 🙏
Thank you for working hard on this game.

developer_response.body: Hey UnidentifiedUser15!  Thank YOU for taking the time to let us know your thoughts, it's honestly gratifying after spending 2 years working on this game (mostly) in secret!

reviews.review_description: Great driving /shooting game. Was having issues and the developers reached out to me to solve the issue the same day. Issues solved. It shows the they care about the game and the gamers who support them. Thanks guys!

developer_response.body:
Hey there!

We were happy to help, but we noticed your review reverted back to 3 stars. :) was that a mistake or did you run into any issues?

reviews.review_description: Issues were addressed, good fun now!

developer_response.body: Hey Waffle.Emperor!  Glad to hear it! there's more on the way!

reviews.review_title: VR FPS SHUMP?!?? Yes. Buy it.
reviews.review_description:
I’ve been playing Japanese “bullet hell” (or shumps or whatever you wanna call them) games since I was a kiddo (Einhander, R-type, Radiant Silvergun, Gradius etc etc) 

This game instills that genre into a first person rail shooter & has a great anime/ early PlayStation era esthetic.

I’ve yet to beat it but If it’s more of what I’ve played in the first 3 levels then bravo. just wanted to show the devs support for showing some love to one of my favorite genres.

Reminds me of a treasure company game (gunstar heroes, Radiant Silvergun) which to me, is a very high compliment. Thanks y’all! Hope to see more games from you.

developer_response.body: Hey alexander.cardosi!  Thanks for the kind word and for understanding our madness.  Yeah, we played a bit too much Gradius, Thunder Force, Darius, et al.  You'll definitely see inspiration from lots of 2-D and 3-D shooters of the era as you play as well.  And yep the game IS difficult, but we're pushing updates constantly to fine tune things so look forward to that as you play!  We've got another update planned (hopefully soon) which will further improve the challenge.  Take care!

reviews.review_title: An absolute gem of a game
reviews.review_description:
In all honesty as of me writing this I'm absolute trash at this game (mainly because I was never particularly good at racing games and my VR shooting skills are abysmal to put it lightly), but I'm slowly getting better and I'm having an absolute blast in the process. It's simple arcade fun, the controls are really intuitive and the difficulty is exactly what I expect from anything inspired by those money draining cabinets we'd visit back in the good old days.

Now I love me some arcade fun, but the main reason I got this game was because of a few words I read on the description "embraces the unique visual style of late 1980’s prestige anime". That's a very bold claim because the late 80s and 90s were the time when both anime and cyberpunk were at their absolute peak and as far as I'm concerned they both started going downhill from there with the rare gem every now and then.

However I am very happy to say that the devs nailed the style and I mean ABSOLUTELY F*CKING NAILED IT. The designs, the use of color and contrast, the mood. the music, it's exactly the kind of stuff I grew up with and love dearly to this very day. Night City can only dream of looking this good, no matter how many extra polygons and raytracings it gets. 
Hell, I expected the music to just be synthwave (and I would've been perfectly fine with that), but instead I got glorious synths and guitars that would be right at home on a PC-Engine CD game. They even got Spike Spiegel and Batou on the voice cast. Honestly I have nothing but respect and praise for the devs for doing such an excellent job of capturing the golden years of anime and my favorite sci-fi subgenre.

TL;DR - Ridiculously fun arcade racing/shooting game that does a phenomenal job of replicating a style that is a lost art nowadays. Devs, release this on Steam already, so I can grab another copy there and shill it to my friends (I'm the only guy with a Quest sadly).

developer_response.body:
Hey Felgerian!
Thanks from the bottom of our hearts for the kind words and the recommendation.  You truly get what we were going for with the look and feel! We honestly tried to go all out with every aspect of the game, and it's honestly worth it to know folks are tuning in to those elements!

 I promise more updates are definitely on the way (and we have additional DLC planned and we have some difficulty tweaks coming as well)!  We actually finished the PC version for the rift, but we want to take some time to add some PC specific features so it's more than a tethered quest version: we want to add elements that will make it ideal for folks who want to stream and share their runs in the highest possible quality. :)

reviews.review_description:
This game is so much fun and is incredibly imaginative. I dig the concept of Akira meets Star Fox it scratches a particular itch. One MAJOR issue with the game in my opinion and an issue in ANY game that's lacking is CUSTOM CONTROLS! This should be common practice in the industry as not every gamer plays the same. I personally don't find the control scheme that the developers had in mind to be suitable although it still gets the job done. Gnarly game!

UPDATE: I'm seriously impressed by the response from the developer and/or PR team. I was not expecting to be informed about my issues with their product and although I still have my problems I at least now have a better understanding as to why the game is designed in such a way. Wicked insightful as I'm not a game designer nor pretend to be one. Quality control and care in the consumer goes a long way.

developer_response.body:
Hey there!  Firstly, thanks for the kind review.  I'm glad to see you kind of get our mid 90's arcade madness, and Star Fox was definitely up there as far as inspiration.  

To touch upon your concern: We definitely know that all players are indeed different.  It's why we've provided some customizability to the controls and accesibility features intended to give you some additional control over your experience.  Truth is the current scheme has honestly been refined to provide the BEST possible experience (seriously).   I realize that many games encourage you to 'grab' to interact with objects, and indeed RUNNER actually started with this implementation, however real-world testing with a diverse group of experienced and novice VR players revealed this was a poor application for our game; it not only resulted in more dropped weapons and unintended interactions, it actually amplified motion sickness, meaning we had to approach this as a problem to solve rather than leaving it to the user to stumble through;

Now, most VR games that allow you to basically fumble about, grabbing things as the primary mode of interaction are generally slow paced or stationary experiences.  This game is a decidedly fast-paced, high-intensity experience where the user is given little to zero time to deal with the limitations of the VR hardware (in the real world, you don't just rely on what you can see, for example, you rely on senses like stereognosis and proprioception that are not present or cannot be adequately simulated in the current iteration of VR).  For that reason, we created a hybrid scheme that focuses the player on the tasks they are going to be spending 90% of their time doing (shooting and driving), with a single-button press modifier to allow them to grab a grenade and use the traditional grip button to throw.  Honestly, I know we can't please everyone here, but trust me when I say this is the best, fastest, and most efficient means of tailoring these interactions for a high-speed driving combat game ;) Honestly, think of it like texting and driving (don't do that, heh): human beings are unable to navigate two sets of complex interfaces efficiently, and having players looking around to grab a grenade whilst trying to avoid traffic and keep tabs on enemies shooting at you can become overwhelming very fast. 

 Because the other system was honestly so clunky in comparison, we really had no reason to implement it as an alternative as it honestly brought more problems to the table, and there was a real risk that players unwilling to try the standard control scheme would have a bad experience otherwise.  Additionally, we needed to rely on some of the buttons that would have to have been reserved for those interactions for other functions (such as enabling the laser sight, arming missiles, etc.)

 Anyway, I hope my reasoning here clears things up a bit, I realize the explanation may have been a bit wordy too but I feel like folks are owed that :) . Feel free to join our discord (linked from our twitter account @runner_vr) if you want to get more into the nitty-gritty of why we've done what we've done, and more importantly what's coming.  take care!

reviews.review_description:
What an arcade! Feels right at home! All 80's babies will appreciate this gem that shines and fires nostalgia! Awesome cyberpunk theme, nostalgic arcade music and intense gameplay, what more could one ask for?? Well maybe some nitro power ups for us adrenaline junkies so we can feel the spike of a good adrenaline rush and a way to go one-wheel so we can celebrate our kills (maybe by pressing acceleration a couple of times?) It would be so awesome! Please consider in a future update! 6 stars

Update (suggestion): Just came into my mind.. How awesome would it be after you rush through each level and beat that boss.. you move into a final fight or cadillacs and dinosaurs type of level but in vr, like a rail shooter (or not, but pretty much straight forward) where you walk through different cyberpunk scenes shooting and killing foes with your guns sword and stuff.. that switch from one play style to another would be cool. I don't know if it is practical and makes sense or simply my mind is overloaded 

Ps: I still pray for the wheelie

developer_response.body: Hey Fat.Friendly.Cat! Thanks for the kind word! We're pretty focused on the motorcycle combat at this point, but we do have some cool bonus stuff on the way, so stay tuned!
